title: US arrest of Kata'ib Hezbollah leader signals shift in approach to Iranian proxy model — Soufan Center analysis
snippet: The Soufan Center's IntelBrief analyzes the recent US arrest of a Kata'ib Hezbollah (KH) leader as a significant shift in Washington's approach to countering Iran's proxy network. The arrest signals a move from broad military strikes toward targeted legal and intelligence operations against Iran-backed militia leadership. The analysis notes KH as the most operationally active Iran-aligned militia in Iraq, with direct links to IRGC Quds Force. The arrest comes amid the Barakah drone attribution to Iraqi territory, suggesting coordinated US pressure on Iraqi-based proxies. The piece argues this represents a more sustainable counter-proxy strategy than aerial bombardment.
tag_reason: 신규 — KH 지도자 체포는 이전 미보도 사건. 이란 프록시 대응 전략 전환(군사→법적/첩보)의 분석적 의미 포함
